title: Middle of the Linked List
prompt:
Given the head of a singly linked list as an array, return the values from the middle node to the end of the list.

If there are two middle nodes (even-length list), return from the second middle node onward.

The input and output are arrays representing linked list values.

examples.output:
[3, 4, 5]
Explanation: The middle node is 3 (index 2 of 5 nodes).

[4, 5, 6]
Explanation: With 6 nodes there are two middles (3 and 4); we return from the second middle.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Use the slow/fast pointer technique. Move slow one step and fast two steps at a time. When fast reaches the end, slow is at the middle. With array input, this simplifies to returning head[len(head)//2:].
commonBugs: Returning the first middle instead of the second for even-length lists
Off-by-one error in calculating the middle index
